observations.recommendation:
1.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary entries
to remove the cost of completed infrastructure projects from the CIP account and
record it to the respective PPE accounts. Accordingly, we also recommended that
depreciation expenses be recognized and computed from the time these
infrastructure assets were reported as completed.

1.9. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer regularly provide the
Municipal Accountant with a copy of the list of completed infrastructure
projects for reference.

2.12. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjusting entries to correct recording errors in the affected accounts from the
General Fund and Trust Fund books, to properly account for the transfer of the
unexpended LDRRM Fund balances. Henceforth, the Municipal
Accountant shall record and make the transfer of any unexpended balance of
LDRRMF (QRF & MOOE) and its corresponding cash, from the General
Fund to Trust Fund, at year-end.

2.13. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed with the
recommendation and committed that adjusting entries will be made on the matter.

3.17. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor authorize the Municipal
Treasurer, with the assistance of the Municipal Accountant, to arrange for the
discontinuance of the time deposit investment of ₱50 Million in the ensuing
month. Henceforth, only those funds which are not allocated for active or
current undertakings, may be considered for passive investment to generate
interest income.

3.18. We also recommended for the Municipal Mayor, together with the members
of the Municipal Planning Development Council and the MPD Coordinator,
to increase their efforts to expedite the implementation of prior and current
years’ unimplemented development PPAs.

3.19. In the exit conference, Management committed to comply with the audit
recommendations.

4.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant communicate with the
DILG to confirm the veracity of the balance of ₱1,750,000.00 pertaining to the
BUB Project 2013 – Potable Water Supply so that information obtained from
the agency can assist the LGU in determining the appropriate action to address
the fund’s full disposition.

4.8. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, by authority from the
Municipal Mayor, draw the check for the unutilized amount of ₱116,813.14 for
its return to the DILG.

4.9. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant committed that they further
verify its records and communicate with DILG on the matter. Also, the Municipal
Treasurer agreed with the recommendation and will comply on the matter.

5.7. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit a written
justification for their decision to move forward with the rice procurement
from supplier DAC, despite the conflict of interest. Henceforth, we
recommended that the BAC be more circumspect in the conduct of its
procurement process, exercising prudence in all its stages, in order to ensure
compliance with laws and regulations.

5.8. In the exit conference, Management, together with the BAC, committed that they
will submit a written justification on the matter and comply with the audit
recommendation. Further, the Municipal Mayor also said that the said BAC
Member took no part in the procurement proceedings with regard to that particular
transaction.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reverse the entries made on derecognized PPEs and provide disclosures on the non-existing/missing PPEs in the Notes to Financial Statements for the ensuing years. The disclosures shall include information on:

The total amount of non-existing/missing PPEs included in the PPE balance, supported with breakdown as to sub-major PPE account group.

Information that a request for authority on the derecognition has been filed and still pending with the COA stating the date of filing thereof, or, still to be filed

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability, based on the list prepared by the Municipal Accountant, to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and Supervising Auditor (SA).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractors who are still covered within the defects liability period to pay the retention money or post its valid substitute.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant deduct “retention money” from payments or require a substitute in the form of   irrevocable standby letters of credit of from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand in future procurements of the LGU to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare correctly the ABCs of upcoming infrastructure projects in conformity with the policies, rules and procedures prescribed in DPWH DO. No. 197, series of 2016, to avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor approves, and the Municipal Budget and Municipal Accountant process payments, for the utilization of the LDRRMF, only those PPAs which are included in the LDRRMFIP and integrated in the AIP to ensure that the transactions are duly authorized to avoid disallowances in audit.

We further recommended that in future transactions, the MDRRMC, through the MDRRMO, incorporate in the annual/revised LDRRMFIP and the Annual/Supplemental Investment Program, all PPAs to be charged to LDRRMF, including subsequent changes thereon, duly supported with proper authority through Resolutions from MDRRMC, MDC and the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant withhold and remit the appropriate amount of taxes from all payments to suppliers by checks, in compliance with BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 and Revenue Regulation No. 11-2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer inform and provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete documents relative to the vaccines received from the DOH and LPHO, as well as its distribution, so that the vaccines will be properly recorded, reported and completely accounted for.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the vaccines received by the LGU, based on the documents submitted by the MHO, as part of its inventory using the perpetual inventory system, and assist the Municipal Health Officer in the proper accounting of the vaccines received, as well as its distribution.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 

Sign a Memorandum of Agreement with government agencies only when all the necessary information is completely incorporated therein, specifically the duration of the implementation of the program to set the parameters of its implementation;

Direct the PESO Manager to conduct thorough profiling of the intended beneficiaries in future programs of the LGU and indicate specific information in the forms to be accomplished; and

Include in the service contracts between the LGU and the beneficiaries all the necessary information to clearly elaborate the terms governing its implementation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

coordinate with the Program Coordinator on the submission to the Audit Team of the needed documents to validate the implementation of the program;

prepare and submit immediately the FUR to DOLE, copy furnished the Audit Team, upon receipt of the lacking documents.

We recommended that the Internal Auditor and the Municipal Accountant continue verifying those PPEs that are subject for reconciliation and make the appropriate adjusting entries in the books of accounts by the end of December 2021.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant process Disbursements Vouchers for the purchase of ancillary supplies, other goods and services only when these are supported with complete documentation, in accordance with GPPB Circular No. 02-2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer inform and provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete documents relative to the vaccines received from the DOH and LPHO, as well as its distribution, so that the vaccines will be properly recorded, reported and completely accounted for.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the vaccines received by the LGU, based on the documents submitted by the MHO, as part of its inventory using the perpetual inventory system, and assist the Municipal Health Officer in the proper accounting of the vaccines received, as well as its distribution.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Sign a Memorandum of Agreement with government agencies only when all the necessary information is completely incorporated therein, specifically the duration of the implementation of the program to set the parameters of its implementation;
Direct the PESO Manager to conduct thorough profiling of the intended beneficiaries in future programs of the LGU and indicate specific information in the forms to be accomplished; and
Include in the service contracts between the LGU and the beneficiaries all the necessary information to clearly elaborate the terms governing its implementation.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
coordinate with the Program Coordinator on the submission to the Audit Team of the needed documents to validate the implementation of the program;
prepare and submit immediately the FUR to DOLE, copy furnished the Audit Team, upon receipt of the lacking documents.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman, through its Secretariat, post in the PhilGEPS and GPPB website the Purchase Order, Contracts and other pertinent documents on the procurement activities of the Municipality of Dulag.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the BAC’s compliance with the on-line posting requirements in Republic Act No. 9184 and other relevant issuances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal entry voucher to take up the understatement of Receivables from RPT and SET amounting to P16,653,254.22 and P18,303,253.34, respectively.  
Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year, with a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, to serve as basis of the latter in recording the RPT and SET receivables in the books.  
Likewise, Management should exert efforts in intensifying collection of long outstanding receivables on RPT and SET, as well as its amounts of accrued interests.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
immediately transfer the unexpended/unobligated balances of the
QRF and DRRMF-MOOE in CY 2016 from the General Fund to
Trust Fund Books.
b. Henceforth, the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer, and
the Municipal Accountant coordinate with each other in updating
monthly the LDRRMF utilization in order to determine and transfer
the unexpended/unobligated balances of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE
from the General Fund to the Trust Fund Books on or before the last
banking day of the current year.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the completed
projects from the Construction in Progress to the Investment, Property Buildings
account.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
reconcile their records with the Offices of the Leyte Provincial
Accountant and Treasurer in order to determine the amount that
is due to the municipality and prepare the necessary adjusting
journal entry.
b. Management make representation with the Leyte Provincial
Government Office to claim the amount due to the municipality.

We recommend that:
a. The personnel in charge at the Office of the Municipal Accountant
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the Accounts
Payable and Due to Officers and Employees accounts with the
supporting detailed analysis of the erroneous accounting entries made.
b. Management, through the Municipal Accountant, strictly adhere with
the provisions of Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommend that:
a. The Office Municipal Accountant prepare the proper accounting
entries to adjust the books of accounts.
b. The Municipal Accountant review the accounting entries prepared
by her staff using as guide the Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009
dated December 1, 2015 and other related issuances.

We recommend that:
a. The Inventory Committee, General Services Officer, Municipal
Engineer and Municipal Accountant comply with the Accounting
and Reporting Procedures enumerated in Item V of COA Circular N0.
2015-008.
b. The General Services Officer maintain a Local Road Inventory and
Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for
all roads and its components.
c. The Municipal Engineer provide the local accountant and the
general services officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.
d. The Local Accountant
d.1 Prepare the Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) in
accordance with Item IX of COA Circular No. 2015-008, and
to maintain the LRNLC for each local road;
d.2 Support the local road network and its components with the
Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads and working paper on
the distribution of costs for the road components; and
d.3 Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network.

We recommend that management ensure the optimal use of the 20%
development fund to help achieve the desired socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of the
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular and other laws, rules and regulations
on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development fund.

We recommend that, management, through the LDRRMO, implement
immediately the projects indicated in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP), as preventive measure to
secure the constituents in times of emergencies caused by disasters.

We recommend that:
a. The GAD Focal Point System meet monthly in order to update the GAD
Fund utilization and accomplishment.
b. Management strictly adhere with Section 36 of R.A. No. 9710 and Section
2.3 of the Joint Circular No. 2012-01 of the PCW, NEDA, and DBM and
other rules and regulations on GAD.

We recommend that management:
a. Strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of Executive Order No.
298.
b. Require the concerned agency officials and personnel to
familiarize with COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29,
2012 in order to guide them on the different types of irregular,
unnecessary, extravagant, excessive and unconscionable
expenditures.
c. Require the concerned participants to refund the excess traveling
expenses totaling P260,028.94.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require the submission
of Official Receipts issued by taxi operators instead of RER for claims for taxi
fares.

We recommend that management:
a. Send immediately at least three (3) consecutive demand letters to the
concerned Accountable Officers, and furnish the COA Auditor with
certified copies duly received by the Accountable Officers.
b. File the appropriate charges against those erring Accountable Officers in
item (a) who failed to liquidate the cash advances despite the 3 demand
letters and furnish the COA Auditor with certified copies.

We recommend that management:
a. Send demand letters monthly to all Accountable Officers requiring
the full settlement of unliquidated cash advances and furnish the COA
Auditor with certified copies duly received by the Accountable
Officers.
b. Suspend the salary of those erring Accountable Officers in item (a)
who failed to liquidate the cash advances despite at least 3 written
demand until such time that they could have fully settled their cash
advances, and furnish the COA Auditor with certified copies of order
of suspension.
c. Update and submit to COA Auditor every 10th day of the ensuing
month the Monitoring of All Cash Advances as of the end of each
month.

We recommend that management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters monthly
to all agency officials and personnel with unliquidated cash advances
for local travel which are unliquidated beyond the prescribed 30-day
period and furnish the COA Auditor with a copy.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
withhold the salary of those with unsettled cash advances for travel
beyond the prescribed period until such time that they have fully
settled their cash advance and refrain from granting new cash
advances to those with unliquidated balances.

We recommend that management:
a. Stop the granting of regular or special purpose cash advances to the
co-terminus employee.
b. Require the accountable officers whose nature of duties require the
possession, custody or control of funds to be bonded and his/her
fidelity insured.

We recommend that:
a. Management reduce the Petty Cash Fund from P20,000.00 to P5,000.00
or P6,000.00.
b. The Municipal Accountant account the Petty Cash Fund under the
imprest system.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the ceiling set in the
DBM National Budget Circulars and other related rules and regulations.

We recommend that:
a. Management pay the salaries, wages, and other monetary benefits of
the agency’s elective officials, regular, casual, contractual and job
order personnel through Automated Teller Machine instead of cash.
b. Management coordinate with nearby agencies and with the AGDB for
the installation of ATMs, and to avail the services of mobile ATM
pending the installation of the machine.

We recommend that:
a. Henceforth, management strictly adhere to the provisions of the
annual General Appropriations Act on the minimum monthly net
take-home pay.
b. Require the concerned officials involved in approving personal loans
to control the loan amount that an employee could avail of by
considering the net monthly net take-home pay.
c. Require the personnel in charge of preparing the payrolls and
disbursement vouchers for the payment of claims for Personnel
Services to use as guide the provisions of the annual General
Appropriations Act in prioritizing the authorized deductions that can
be charged against the employees’ claims for Personnel Services,
subject to the monthly net take-home pay limitation.
d. Management, through the Office of the Municipal Accountant, inform
in writing the concerned employees on authorized and other
deductions that can no longer be deducted from their salaries due to
monthly net take-home pay limitation.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Accountant update the subsidiary accounting
records and furnish the Municipal Treasurer with a certified copy
thereof at the end of each month.
b. The Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balances with the
subsidiary accounting records every end of the month in strict
adherence to the provisions of Section 63 (F) of the Local Treasury
Operations Manual.
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit to
the Auditor the Reconciliation Statements monthly.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer submit to the Auditor on the fifth day of the
following month the Consolidated Report of Accountability for
Accountable Forms and the supporting Reports of Accountability for
Accountable Forms.
b. The Municipal Treasurer include all accountable forms including
checks in the RAAF and CRAAF.
c. Management assign additional personnel to the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Local Accountant a copy of the
Inventory and the Statement of Accountability for Accountable
Forms, Plates and Stickers at the end of the month and at year-end.
b. The Municipal Accountant familiarize with the Revised Chart of
Accounts for Local Government Units and to record properly in the
books the procurement and issuance of all accountable forms.
c. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant coordinate
with each other to reconcile their records on all accountable forms.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer submit weekly the financial transaction
documents and reports to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
b. The Municipal Accountant immediately record in the books of
accounts the reports and financial transaction documents from the
Office of the Municipal Treasurer and submit these documents with
the journals and other required reports to the COA Auditor within the
first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.
c. Management assign additional personnel to the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
